description: Authoritative conceptual-tier and Codex-adapter mapping (2026-07-18 evidence-receipted retier; per-role table in PR #74). DeepSWE v1.1 + the sol effort research set the lanes: Sol/medium is the measured workhorse (61.1% pass@1 at $1.86/task, OpenAI's own Codex default); Sol/high (~1.9x medium's quota burn) is reserved for judgment that gates other work (strategy, review verdicts, architecture); above high the marginal quality per credit collapses, so Sol/xhigh is reserved for exactly one rare high-consequence lane, wsh-security-auditor. Luna/xhigh (56.9% at $1.54/task) is the budget lane for BOUNDED work whose output is verified downstream and whose per-task context stays small -- Luna's long-context recall cliff (41.3%) bars anything that reads large diffs or whole codebases. Its members and why they clear that bar: muster-surgeon (1-2 file mechanical edits), wsh-api-documenter and wsh-tutorial-engineer (doc recipes over given sources), wsh-test-automator (module-scoped authoring against a minimal dispatch brief, self-verified by running the suite, and deliberately a different model family than the Sol builders it checks -- METR flags Sol's reward-hack rate highest on verifier-adjacent work), and the human-read content quartet wsh-business-analyst/wsh-content-marketer/wsh-customer-support/wsh-data-scientist (bounded inputs, human-reviewed output). On quota-billed plans the Luna lane also spends a separate ~3x larger message allowance, preserving Sol quota. Terra/high ($1.13 at 53.8%, long-context safe) is reserved for the read-only locator muster-investigator. Fable remains conceptual and adapts to Sol/high, never routine max; muster-strategist stays Sol/high.
